data.body:
PASS 1 review — T11 (Data-migration / backfill safety) [overlay]: NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS.

This overlay applies only when a plan changes a schema/persisted format or backfills stored data. This epic is a test-coverage / contract-test initiative: the "snapshot-entry schema module" is a code-level shape contract for validating in-memory snapshot dicts (not a persisted DB schema), and the "fixtures derived from real Jira payloads" are hermetic test fixtures (not a production data backfill). The substantive code changes — collapsing dual applier paths, adding sub-op telemetry, round-trip contract tests, honest probes — are test-infrastructure/code-path changes with no persisted-format migration or stored-record backfill. Per the T11 ANTI-FP rule ("not-applicable for non-persisted/in-memory changes") no finding is raised. Affirmed clean for T11.
